They'll still exist in terms of having predictable revenue, but usage-based pricing helps you unlock revenue growth and potential that's not tied to number of users.

And I think a lot of companies, especially with AI, don't know how to price those products, and they're having to change how their entire business operates, whether they're, like, an early-stage company, like, monetizing AI, or if it's, like, a traditional SaaS company who is building AI into their products.

I think there's not, there's a lot of, like, high-level thought leadership on pricing, which we also want to dominate, but I think there's also a lot of, like, the in-practice stuff that people are really hungry for, and we've seen, like, validation with that from a lot of the, like, webinars or concepts.

I think, I think it depends on the size and stage of company.
I think like earlier stage, growth stage companies, the conversation a lot of times is like more product and engineering because the finance function isn't as mature.

I think one of our main areas that we're like coming up head to head with Stripe on is like transforming companies, so we kind of define that as like traditional subscription companies that are either transforming their business model to be usage-based focused, or they're launching like a new product or like an add-on that is usage-based or hybrid, and I think for those companies that are more established, like finance becomes more of a voice because they have existing systems in place and like integrations that they've built out.
Like, I think from a product perspective, our finance story hasn't.

I strong, but I think finance folks are looking for a lot of information on what does Revruck look like in this world, how do I think about forecasting, like how does this like help me with my month-end close process, like there's a lot of information that they're seeking from us, and just like in general the industry that there's a gap on, and so even though our product might not be fully there, I think there's a lot of information we can share about like how Confluent or Databricks like are able to power month-end close with Metronome to kind of like de-risk that for them.

I think for product and engineering, I think engineering is more they don't want to maintain this infrastructure, and so how can they use our APIs or our out-of-box integrations to like kind of offload billing so that product owners or business owners can make those pricing and changes without them and they can focus on the parts of the integration that they actually need to be involved with.
I think for product, product,
I think the main thing is, like, launching products on time and have billing not be a bottleneck.

I think some of the customers we talk to, it's not like billing is thought about as, like, a last mile thing when you're launching a new product, but it actually is, like, a huge pain and can cause delays.
So if they're coming to us, it's usually, like, they have a product launch imminently and they realize, like, their billing system can't do it and they might have to buy.

But I think another piece that is, a requirement for them with usage-based pricing is that they need to build billing into their product experience so that customers can know in real-time how much they're spending, because that's directly correlated to usage.
So I think it's also, like, customer experience and building in that real-timiness is what product leaders need to have and that we can help with.
Yeah, I'd say, like, in a perfect world, we could narrow down our personas to, like, one specific focus, but the space we're in, like, billing can be owned by a lot

Different functions, it depends on the company size, depends on the structure, but like there's not super often just like a clear billing team that you can go after, and so you do have to take these like different entry points, and I would say like, and Maggie, tell me if I'm wrong, but I feel like if we absolutely had to prioritize and put the weight behind the most important here, probably for this transforming thing, it sits under product like the most heavily.

Like, you all have a role to play in billing.
